label: Empirical scaling laws

node.label: Empirical scaling laws
node.definition: Empirical scaling laws fit predictable relationships between loss or capability and model size, data, training compute, or inference compute.
node.why_it_matters: They make expensive model development partly forecastable and turn scaling into an explicit resource-allocation problem.
limitations: Fits can break across architectures, data regimes, objectives, or task metrics and are not universal physical laws.

dependencies.rationale: Empirical scaling laws rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Statistics & experimental design.
Empirical scaling laws rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Information theory.
Empirical scaling laws rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Optimization theory.
Empirical scaling laws rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Complexity & computability.
